title: Memory of Processes
description: Covers memory allocation, paging, segmentation, and page tables.

questions.prompt: Which hardware component translates logical (virtual) addresses into physical addresses?
Which of the following are TRUE about contiguous memory allocation?
options: Each process occupies a single continuous section of memory
Suffers from external fragmentation (total space exists but is scattered)
Requires base and limit registers in the MMU
Compaction can combine free holes but has high overhead

questions.prompt: Match each variable-partition allocation strategy to its behaviour.
right: Picks the smallest hole that is big enough
Picks the first hole that is big enough

questions.prompt: In paging, what is the name for a fixed-size block of a program?
In paging, what is the name for a fixed-size block of physical memory?
Which of the following are TRUE about the Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB)?
options: Hardware cache of recently-used page table entries
On a hit, address translation takes one memory access
On a miss, two memory accesses are still required
The ASID allows multiple processes to share the TLB safely

questions.prompt: Match each page table structure to its defining characteristic.
right: One entry per physical frame, indexed by (PID, page number); small but slow to search
Outer table indexes into inner tables; invalid regions can be collapsed into one entry
Page number is hashed; colliding entries stored in a linked list

questions.prompt: What is the name for an access to a logical address that has no corresponding physical frame, requiring a fetch from secondary storage?
Which of the following are TRUE about segmentation?
options: A program is divided into variable-size logical segments
Logical addresses have the form (segment number, offset)
The segment table stores a base and limit for each segment
Accessing beyond the segment limit raises an addressing error
